name: Vercel Home
strong: Restrained, premium, technically credible, and very intentional.
weak: Can feel too austere for brands that need more warmth.
avoid: copying the exact coldness for warmer brands
copying brand-specific assets
why_this_matters: A high-signal example of premium product marketing through discipline rather than decoration.
reusable_lesson: Better hierarchy and spacing beat extra visual effects.

name: Vercel Web Interface Guidelines
strong: Practical quality guidance for polished, fast, and accessible interfaces.
weak: Not a brand reference by itself; needs translation into a product context.
avoid: treating guidelines as a finished visual direction
why_this_matters: It encodes quality standards that can improve almost any modern web interface.
reusable_lesson: Good design quality is often systemized through small rules, not big visual flourishes.

name: Linear Home
strong: Calm, premium, product-led, and highly controlled.
weak: Too much imitation can make other products feel colder than necessary.
avoid: copying the exact mood when the product needs more human warmth
why_this_matters: A benchmark for premium product marketing and operational product feel.
reusable_lesson: Premium product surfaces feel quiet and controlled, not busy.

name: Linear UI Redesign
strong: Explains how strong teams test hierarchy, chrome, and contrast before shipping.
weak: It is process-heavy rather than a direct visual reference.
avoid: treating redesign as purely cosmetic
why_this_matters: Shows a mature product-design process for app-wide UX improvements.
reusable_lesson: Strong redesigns define behavior and hierarchy across the system, not just one screen.

name: Stripe Home
strong: Confident, premium, and exceptionally strong at proof and scale signals.
weak: The visual system is category-specific and can overpower simpler products if copied too literally.
avoid: using fintech-specific visual cues in unrelated products
overcomplicating smaller product pages
why_this_matters: A world-class example of high-trust conversion design.
reusable_lesson: Strong proof and strong hierarchy can feel premium without feeling salesy.

name: Mercury Home
strong: Quiet confidence, premium trust, and polished clarity.
weak: Some trust patterns are tuned for finance rather than general SaaS or services.
avoid: finance-specific cues when the product is not financial
why_this_matters: Shows how to feel premium and safe at the same time.
reusable_lesson: Premium trust comes from polish and clarity working together.

name: Raycast Home
strong: Expressive while still feeling premium and product-led.
weak: Can drift toward richer visual storytelling than some enterprise or service products need.
avoid: adding visual richness without enough product clarity
why_this_matters: Useful when a product needs more personality without falling into noise.
reusable_lesson: You can add energy without sacrificing control if hierarchy stays strong.

name: Framer Home
strong: Modern, designer-facing, and strong at visual pacing.
weak: Its visual richness can be too much for more utilitarian products.
avoid: over-designing simple business products
why_this_matters: A strong reference for more design-forward landing pages.
reusable_lesson: Visual richness only works when composition remains disciplined.

name: Apple Design
strong: Canonical clarity, restraint, and platform-level polish.
weak: Needs adaptation outside the Apple product context.
avoid: blindly applying Apple-specific conventions to non-Apple products
why_this_matters: A timeless reference for high-quality interface judgment.
reusable_lesson: Clarity and polish are often the result of subtraction, not addition.

name: Apple Design Resources
strong: Helpful for platform-specific fidelity.
weak: More resource-oriented than broad visual inspiration.
avoid: using licensed resources outside their intended context
why_this_matters: Useful when designing Apple-platform experiences with higher native fidelity.
reusable_lesson: Platform resources are valuable when used with respect for context and license.

name: Shopify Polaris
strong: Operational clarity, strong system thinking, and practical component discipline.
weak: A system reference, not automatically a premium marketing reference.
avoid: treating a design system as a full brand direction
why_this_matters: Excellent for improving serious product surfaces and dashboards.
reusable_lesson: Great admin UX comes from consistency and clarity more than decoration.

name: Intercom Inbox
strong: Strong shared-workspace logic and communication-centered UX.
weak: Can feel specific to support workflows if copied too literally.
avoid: bringing support-specific interaction patterns into unrelated products
why_this_matters: A strong reference for any app that mixes queue, conversation, and action.
reusable_lesson: Shared work feels faster when state, ownership, and actions live together.

name: Cal.com Teams
strong: Clear scheduling logic and strong next-step clarity.
weak: Scheduling-specific patterns do not transfer everywhere.
avoid: forcing calendar-first patterns into non-scheduling experiences
why_this_matters: Useful for products that route people toward appointments or meetings.
reusable_lesson: Booking UX improves when the user understands who, why, and what happens next.

name: Ramp Home
strong: Strong trust, clear value articulation, and operational seriousness.
weak: Some visual language may be too finance-specific for other categories.
avoid: carrying over finance-specific signals into unrelated products
why_this_matters: Helpful for products that need to feel controlled, serious, and trustworthy.
reusable_lesson: Operational credibility should be visible in the UI, not hidden behind marketing gloss.

name: Figma Home
strong: Excellent product storytelling and broad design maturity.
weak: The brand is stronger and more colorful than some minimalist products want.
avoid: borrowing visual loudness when a quieter system is needed
why_this_matters: Useful when a product needs to explain a rich workflow clearly.
reusable_lesson: Complex products feel easier when the story is paced well.

name: Notion Home
strong: Friendly, calm, and broad without losing clarity.
weak: Can feel softer and more editorial than tighter premium systems.
avoid: softening a product that needs more authority or density
why_this_matters: A useful reference for making complex products feel approachable.
reusable_lesson: Approachability can coexist with polish when the layout stays disciplined.

name: Notion AI
strong: AI positioned as a product capability instead of generic hype.
weak: The tone may be too broad for more technical AI products.
avoid: vague AI claims with no workflow context
why_this_matters: A good model for AI positioning that feels useful instead of gimmicky.
reusable_lesson: AI pages feel stronger when the capability is grounded in real workflows.

name: ChatGPT Overview
strong: Clear capability framing and broad consumer-to-pro positioning.
weak: Some patterns are tuned for a very broad audience.
avoid: generic assistant copy without clear value to the target user
why_this_matters: Useful for understanding AI product positioning at scale.
reusable_lesson: Broad AI products still need very clear entry points and capability hierarchy.

name: Apps in ChatGPT
strong: Good for understanding ecosystem and platform UX framing.
weak: Article-style reference more than a canonical visual benchmark.
avoid: treating announcement pages as complete product-style references
why_this_matters: Useful when positioning extensible AI platforms rather than single-use tools.
reusable_lesson: Platform experiences need a different narrative than standalone product pages.

name: OpenAI Codex
strong: Clear multi-surface product framing and strong value articulation for builders.
weak: Builder-focused tone may be too technical for broader products.
avoid: over-technical framing for non-technical audiences
why_this_matters: A strong reference for AI products aimed at builders and technical teams.
reusable_lesson: AI builder products feel stronger when the workflow is explicit, not abstract.

name: Claude Code
strong: Focused positioning for an AI coding workflow.
weak: Some pages in this category can lean heavy on concept over concrete UI evidence.
avoid: too much abstraction without concrete UI or workflow support
why_this_matters: Useful for understanding how serious AI developer tools present themselves.
reusable_lesson: Developer-facing AI products need workflow specificity, not just capability claims.

name: Cursor Home
strong: Strong builder positioning and product-led AI framing.
weak: Can inherit category sameness if interpreted too generically.
avoid: copying category tropes instead of product-specific clarity
why_this_matters: Relevant for AI developer product references.
reusable_lesson: Even hot AI categories need product-specific clarity to avoid sameness.

name: v0 Home
product_type: AI design and coding tool marketing
strong: Clear prompt-to-output positioning for a design-forward AI tool.
weak: Some AI-builder aesthetics can drift toward category sameness.
avoid: generic AI styling without differentiated product proof
why_this_matters: Relevant for AI tools that blend design, code, and fast iteration.
reusable_lesson: AI creation tools need concrete outcome framing more than generic intelligence claims.

name: Lovable Home
strong: Clear promise and accessible builder positioning.
weak: This category often risks visual sameness and hype if not handled carefully.
avoid: category-level hype with weak proof
why_this_matters: Useful for understanding how AI app-builders position themselves to broad audiences.
reusable_lesson: AI builder products need stronger proof to avoid feeling interchangeable.

name: Perplexity Home
strong: Clear category leadership signal and strong mainstream AI recognition.
weak: The homepage itself may reveal less visual detail through scraping than other references.
avoid: using a thin scrape as if it were a full visual reference
why_this_matters: Useful as an AI category reference even when harvest depth is lighter.
reusable_lesson: Not every strong product produces equally rich scrape artifacts; depth of evidence matters.
